description: Two gardeners work on separate beds but time their watering so the shared tank does not run dry. 'molawi' means working so that one's actions fit with other participants' actions towards an understood outcome. The effort may be unequal or tense; cooperation describes how the actions fit, not whether the arrangement is fair.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced 'm', let the resonance pass through the nose, and open into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and release along its sides into stressed open central unrounded 'a'. Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into high-front 'i'.
usage_notes: A plural subject can cooperate, and reciprocal 'wiso' makes mutual action explicit. The event-noun rule supplies cooperation. 'wenola' (collaborate) joins participants in shaping one undertaking, while Work module 'wephari' (coordinate) arranges dependencies among activities.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared gardens and repair work often depend on people adjusting separate tasks around common resources. Cooperation names that practical relation; control remains a separate question.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: People can cooperate on a bounded task while their disagreement or mistrust remains visible.

semantic_domains.activity: adjusting separate actions so they work compatibly
semantic_domains.community: cooperation among participants towards an understood outcome
